<!-- source-hash: d1f50ebf1a26783de1bbe162f0a9ca79 -->
REST controller exposing CRUD and filtering operations for events under the `/api/v1/events` base path, with cursor-based pagination and OpenAPI documentation annotations.

## Key Components

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `getEvents` | `GET /api/v1/events` | Retrieves a paginated, filterable, sortable list of events |
| `getEvent` | `GET /api/v1/events/{id}` | Fetches a single event by ID; throws `EventNotFoundException` if missing |
| `createEvent` | `POST /api/v1/events` | Creates a new event and returns `201 CREATED` |
| `updateEvent` | `PUT /api/v1/events/{id}` | Replaces an existing event by ID |
| `getEventFilters` | `GET /api/v1/events/filters` | Returns available filter options (user IDs, event types, date ranges) |

**Dependencies:**
- `EventService` — business logic layer for querying, creating, and updating events
- `EventMapper` — maps domain `Event` objects to external-facing DTOs
- `CursorPaginationCriteria` — encapsulates cursor + limit for keyset pagination
- `SortInput` — wraps sort field and direction
- `EventFilterCriteria` — groups user IDs, event types, and date range filters

## Usage Example

```java
// GET /api/v1/events?userIds=u1,u2&eventTypes=LOGIN&startDate=2024-01-01&limit=50&sortDirection=DESC
// Headers: X-API-Key-Id: ak_xxx

// GET /api/v1/events/{id}
// Returns 404 with ErrorResponse if event not found

// POST /api/v1/events
// Body: Event JSON payload
// Returns 201 with EventResponse

// PUT /api/v1/events/{id}
// Body: Updated Event JSON payload
// Returns 200 with EventResponse

// GET /api/v1/events/filters
// Returns EventFilterResponse with available filter options
```

> **Note:** All endpoints accept optional `X-User-Id` and `X-API-Key-Id` request headers for audit logging. Pagination uses cursor-based (keyset) strategy; `limit` is bounded between 1 and 100, defaulting to 20.
